metadata.methodology: Ecological quality status is Water Framework Directive assessment for chalk-dominated river water bodies in England. Sewage events are storm overflow discharges recorded via Environment Agency Event Duration Monitoring (EDM) on catchments feeding chalk streams. The 95% in poor condition figure comes from the Chalk Stream Restoration Group's 2021 assessment. England holds 85% of world's chalk streams — approximately 160 major chalk rivers covering roughly 4,000 km.
knownIssues: WFD ecological classification uses a 'one out, all out' rule — failure on any one element (chemistry, biology, hydrology) results in classification as less than good, making 100% good status a very high bar.
EDM sewage event data improved significantly in coverage from 2016 as more monitors were installed — the apparent increase in events partly reflects better measurement, not solely deterioration.
Chalk stream boundaries are not standardised — different organisations classify slightly different river lengths as chalk streams.
